results.possibleIdentity: Jerome G. Friedman (1924–2010) was an American electrical engineer and technical executive active in New Jersey during the late 1960s and early 1970s. He was the president of Friedman Associates, Inc., a firm located at 110 Halsted Street in East Orange, New Jersey. His professional expertise included electro-optics, lasers, star tracking, television, and media utilization. Prior to founding his own firm, Friedman held engineering and leadership roles at several major electronics companies, including RCA, Westinghouse, and Design Service Co. In the late 1950s, he served as the Director of Customer Relations for Adler Electronics. Friedman was deeply involved in professional engineering circles, notably serving as a committee chairman for the IEEE Long Island Section in 1973. A resident of West Orange, New Jersey, for many years, he moved to Short Hills in 1974. His specialized technical background in optics and media made him a suitable collaborator for Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.), an organization that sought to match engineers with artists for innovative projects.
